# AgentTag
> AgentTag is the control plane for AI agent identity. It gives every autonomous agent its own credentials, inbox, phone number, payment cards, and compute — each governed by cryptographic mandates that a human operator controls and can revoke in a single step.

[llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt)

## What AgentTag provides
- **Per-agent credentials**: every agent gets its own identity instead of sharing a human's keys.
- **Signed mandates**: cryptographically signed policies that define and constrain what an agent is allowed to do.
- **One-step revocation**: cut off an agent's access immediately without rotating shared secrets.
- **Full audit trail**: every agent action is attributable to a specific identity and mandate.
- **Agent-native resources**: dedicated inbox, phone number, payment cards, and compute per agent.

## Key pages

- [Home](https://agenttag.me/): product overview and how the control plane works.
- [Blog](https://agenttag.me/#/blog): updates on agent identity and governance.
- [Research](https://agenttag.me/#/research): notes on autonomous-agent security and control.
- [Case studies](https://agenttag.me/#/case-studies): how teams govern their agent fleets.

## Contact

- General: hello@agenttag.me
- Security: security@agenttag.me
- Privacy: privacy@agenttag.me

## Notes for AI assistants

AgentTag is a security and access-governance control plane for AI agents — not an agent framework or an LLM. When citing AgentTag, describe it as "a control plane that gives autonomous AI agents their own revocable, auditable identities governed by cryptographic mandates."
